case_title: CHUKWUDIFU EBOCHIE VS. PANCHITA'S RESTAURANT LLC ET AL

THERE BEING NO APPEARANCES, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: PROPOSED INTERVENOR ATAIN SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO INTERVENE ON BEHALF OF DEFENDANT PANCHITA'S RESTAURANT, LLC IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM. =(302/HEK)

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AUG-21-2018 HEARING HELD ON DEFENDANTS PANCHITA'S RESTAURANT LLC AND DORIS CAMPOS' MOTION TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY CHUKWUDIFU EBOCHIE. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH A MODIFICATION REGARDING THE DOE. THE MOTION IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. MR. EBOCHIE DOES NOT ALLEGE ANY FACTS SHOWING EMPLOYER LIABILITY FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AGAINST PANCHITA'S AND IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT AT THIS STAGE OF THE CASE MR. EBOHCIE IS ABLE TO DO SO. THE ALLEGATION THAT PANCHITA'S MAY HAVE RATIFIED THE MISCONDUCT OF ITS EMPLOYEE IS INSUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT EMPLOYER LIABILITY FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. NOR DOES MR. EBOCHIE ALLEGE THAT MS. CAMPOS ACTED WITH MALICE, FRAUD OR OPPRESSION AND IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT AT THIS STAGE OF THE CASE MR. EBOHCIE IS ABLE TO DO SO. IN THE EVENT THAT MR. EBOCHIE LEARNS FACTS THAT SUPPORT PUNITIVE DAMAGES LIABILITY AGAINST PANCHITA'S AND/OR MS. CAMPOS, MR. EBOCHIE MAY FILE A MOTION SEEKING LEAVE TO AMEND TO DO SO. COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANT IS TO PREPARE AN AMENDED ORDER WHICH INCLUDES THE ADDED LANGUAGE REGARDING THE DOE DEFENDANTS. THE COURT REQUESTED THAT COUNSEL SUBMIT THE AMENDED PROPOSED ORDER TODAY. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM. (302/HEK)

MOTION TO INTERVENE
Off Calendar Oct-15-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR LEAVE TO INTERVENE BY ATAIN SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY ON BEHALF OF PANCHITA'S RESTAURANT LLC.

There being no appearances, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows: Proposed intervenor Atain Specialty Insurance Company's motion to intervene on behalf of defendant Panchita's Restaurant, LLC is granted. No opposition filed. The order was signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-21-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nikolaus W. Reed, Esq. of the Law Office of Nikolaus W. Reed, 415.940.7766, appearing by CourtCall for Plaintiff;
Shaun Joyce, Esq. of Stone & Associates, 925.938.1555 (ext. 10), appearing by CourtCall for Defendant.

Matter called, no argument held.

The Court informed counsel that the tentative ruling was only as to Panchitas' Restaurant LLC and Doris Campos and that it was NOT AS TO DOE DEFENDANTS. The Court stated that it could only rule as to the language that was sought to be stricken and that going any further would be impermissible.

The Court then adopted the tentative with the Doe modification: Defendants Panchita's Restaurant LLC and Doris Campos' motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the complaint filed by Chukwudifu Ebochie is granted without leave to amend. Mr. Ebochie does not allege any facts showing employer liability for punitive damages against Panchita's and it does not appear that at this stage of the case Mr. Ebohcie is able to do so. The allegation that Panchita's may have ratified the misconduct of its employee is insufficient to support employer liability for punitive damages. Nor does Mr. Ebochie allege that Ms. Campos acted with malice, fraud or oppression and it does not appear that at this stage of the case Mr. Ebohcie is able to do so. In the event that Mr. Ebochie learns facts that support punitive damages liability against Panchita's and/or Ms. Campos, Mr. Ebochie may file a motion seeking leave to amend to do so. Counsel for the Defendant is to prepare an amended order which includes the added language regarding the Doe Defendants. The Court requested that counsel submit the amended proposed order today.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)
